rows.notes: Clear centered page title in bold script at very top. No monetary columns present on this line; alignment and text are unambiguous.

rows.description: Interest for one yr on Shuttle's deposit 1804
rows.notes: Amount columns clearly aligned to right; pounds column reads '6' with two trailing zeros for s and d. The wording 'one yr' is abbreviated but legible. Vertical ruling lines used to place 6|0|0.

rows.description: To Mr Smith a bill for Doct Gutters
rows.notes: Pounds '5' clearly sits between pounds and shillings lines; shillings and pence read '0 0'. The recipient name and purpose are readable but the abbreviation 'Doct' and exact surname spelling are slightly uncertain from the handwriting.

rows.description: To Do a moiety of Cost of a new Paving
rows.notes: Amount '4 0 0' is well aligned and clear. The description contains a word that is faint/ornate (interpreted as 'Paving' or similar); 'Do' indicates same payee as previous line. Because the text is somewhat stylized, confidence in exact word choice is moderate.

rows.description: To the Subrector for the Liquidation Fund for the year 1828
rows.notes: Large pounds figure '200' clearly aligned under pounds column (three-digit number readable). The office name 'Subrector' and phrase 'Liquidation Fund' are legible though the title spelling is slightly archaic; amounts validate correctly.

rows.description: To Do for the Augmentation of L. Whitehead for the years 1829 & 1830
rows.notes: Pounds '100' is clearly written and aligned. The line begins with 'Do' (same payee) and mentions 'Augmentation' and a proper name (interpreted as 'L. Whitehead' from cursive letters); slight uncertainty on exact given initial but context supports reading.

rows.description: To Mr Dayman for repairs of Carriage & Cottages at Little Tew
rows.notes: Amount '1 14 6' aligns well in the three narrow columns; shillings '14' and pence '6' are clear. The payee name looks like 'Dayman' rather than alternatives (handwriting of D and y is stylized), and place 'Little Tew' is legible; moderate confidence in exact spelling of some words.

rows.description: To Faulkner Sundry Bills for repairs at South ?avington
rows.notes: Monetary columns '4 14 6' are clear and valid. The recipient surname appears to be 'Faulkner' (legible) and 'Sundry Bills for repairs' is readable; the place name after 'at' is faint/ornate and uncertain (rendered as '?avington'), so description text carries moderate ambiguity.

rows.description: To Mr Underwood for designs for Buildings at the Back Gate
rows.notes: Amount '5 0 0' is unambiguous. 'Underwood' and 'designs for Buildings' read clearly; the place is read as 'Back Gate' though alternative wording could be 'Rack Gate'—chosen based on letter shapes; moderate confidence on that word.

rows.description: To the Subrector for College Plate from Messrs Lambert & Rawlings
rows.notes: Amount '35 9 0' aligns with pounds/shillings/pence columns and is clear. 'Subrector' and 'College Plate' are legible; the suppliers 'Lambert & Rawlings' read reasonably distinctly though ampersand and final name flourish required interpretation.

rows.description: To Do for Sundries as pr. Acct.
rows.notes: Amount '2 7 0' is clear and columns are well aligned. The text begins 'To Do' (same payee) and reads 'Sundries as pr. Acct.' in standard ledger shorthand; legible and contextually consistent.

rows.description: To Mr Davis - Rent of the Chapel-Barn at Little Tew 1 1/2 yrs
rows.notes: Amount '6 0 0' is unambiguous. The rent description reads clearly; the fractional tenancy '1 1/2 yrs' is handwritten but legible. Slight uncertainty only in interpreting the fractional-year abbreviation.

rows.description: To Do a Bill for Bricks at S. Newton Farm
rows.notes: Amount '3 2 9' is clear. 'S. Newton Farm' is legible; 'Bill for Bricks' reading is straightforward. 'Do' indicates same payee as previous line.

rows.description: To Do for Oak planks at Hartley's
rows.notes: Amount '1 11 0' is well aligned and readable. 'Oak planks' and the place/supplier (interpreted as 'Hartley's') are legible though the final letter has an ornamental flourish; moderate confidence in exact name spelling.

rows.description: To Messrs Cox & Co Insurance for Hill School
rows.notes: Amount reads '1 11 11' in the three narrow columns; pence '11' is within valid range. The supplier looks like 'Cox & Co' (common firm name) and 'Insurance for Hill School' reads plausibly; slight uncertainty due to tight script but amounts are clear.

rows.description: To Mr Parker for Prayer Books in Chapel
rows.notes: Monetary columns '4 16 0' are clear and valid. 'Parker' and 'Prayer Books in Chapel' are easily readable; alignment confirms the values.
Amount '17 15 2' is well aligned and legible. The surname 'Grove' and phrase 'Revision 2 yrs' plus the years '1830 & 1831' are readable though the small script of the years required close inspection.

rows.description: To Taylor - Bill for repairs at Becks Farm
rows.notes: Amount '5 14 10' reads clearly in the three-column alignment. The payee 'Taylor' is clear; the farm/place name is slightly ambiguous in the handwriting (rendered as 'Becks Farm' but could be similar), hence moderate confidence on the description.
